edges.description: Height-adjusted total kidney volume is an imaging readout of cumulative cyst burden and progressive kidney enlargement; larger and faster-growing TKV predicts steeper eGFR decline and earlier end-stage renal disease, supporting TKV as a prognostic enrichment biomarker for ADPKD trial patient selection, not a validated treatment-efficacy surrogate.

nodes.description: Increased urinary albumin excretion, an early marker of kidney damage in ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012592 | Albuminuria | Frequent (79-30%)
urine studies for microalbuminuria or proteinuria every one to five years in adults depending on disease stage

nodes.description: Aortic root dilatation and risk of thoracic aortic dissection reported in ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002616 | Aortic root aneurysm | Occasional (29-5%)
dilatation of the aortic root and dissection of the thoracic aorta; mitral valve prolapse; and abdominal wall hernias

nodes.description: Arachnoid membrane cysts as a rare extrarenal cystic manifestation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0100702 | Arachnoid cyst | Occasional (29-5%)
cysts in the pancreas, seminal vesicles, and arachnoid membrane

nodes.description: Progressive decline in kidney function, with approximately 50% reaching end-stage renal disease by age 62.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012622 | Chronic kidney disease | Frequent (79-30%)
Approximately 50% of individuals with ADPKD require kidney replacement therapy by 62 years of age
The change from baseline in the estimated GFR was -2.34 ml per minute per 1.73 m2

nodes.description: Primary cilia act as mechanosensors in renal tubular epithelial cells. Polycystin dysfunction impairs ciliary signaling, disrupting normal tubular architecture and promoting cyst formation through increased cAMP signaling.

evidence.snippet: PC-1 is a membrane receptor that localizes to the primary cilium in renal epithelial cells
The localization of the PC-1/PC-2 complex in the primary cilium is proposed to be crucial for its function as a mechanosensory antenna of fluid flow in the kidney.

nodes.description: Ductal plate malformation leading to periportal fibrosis and bile duct proliferation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002612 | Congenital hepatic fibrosis | Frequent (79-30%)
a ductal plate malformation of the liver resulting in congenital hepatic fibrosis

nodes.description: Progressive bilateral kidney enlargement due to cyst growth, quantifiable by total kidney volume.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000105 | Enlarged kidney | Occasional (29-5%)
Patients with MIC 1C to MIC 1E have larger kidneys because of more rapid growth (6%-10% per year)

nodes.description: Reduced polycystin-mediated restraint on epithelial proliferation leads to progressive cyst wall growth and parenchymal compression, increasing total kidney volume and reducing renal function.

evidence.reference_title: Tolvaptan in Later-Stage Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease.
evidence.snippet: slowed the growth in total kidney volume and the decline in the estimated glomerular filtration rate

nodes.description: Progressive cyst growth triggers interstitial inflammation and fibrosis. Activated myofibroblasts deposit extracellular matrix, contributing to nephron loss and declining kidney function.

evidence.snippet: Macrophage recruitment and interstitial inflammation promote cyst growth.

nodes.description: Chronic or acute pain due to cyst hemorrhage, infection, or mass effect.

evidence.snippet: HP:0030157 | Flank pain | Frequent (79-30%)
Kidney manifestations include early-onset hypertension, kidney pain, and kidney insufficiency.
Comorbidities include hypertension, flank pain, and bacterial infections.

nodes.description: Gross or microscopic hematuria from cyst hemorrhage, occurring in 50-60% of ADPKD patients.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000790 | Hematuria | Frequent (79-30%)
characterized by progressive outgrowths of fluid-filled cysts from the renal epithelium, which can manifest with hematuria, urinary tract infections, hypertension, and abdominal or flank pain
Cyst hemorrhage and/or gross hematuria usually responds to bed rest, analgesics, and adequate hydration.

nodes.description: Liver cysts are the most common extrarenal manifestation, present in >90% of patients over 35, more prevalent in women.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001407 | Hepatic cysts | Very frequent (99-80%)
More than 90% of patients older than 35 years have hepatic cysts, which may cause abdominal discomfort and occasionally require medical or surgical intervention
The prevalence of liver cysts increases with age and occasionally results in clinically significant severe polycystic liver disease (PLD), most often in females.

nodes.description: Congenital hepatic fibrosis is a hallmark of ARPKD, resulting from ductal plate malformation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001395 | Hepatic fibrosis | Very frequent (99-80%)
characterized by cystic dilatation and ectasia of renal collecting tubules, and a ductal plate malformation of the liver resulting in congenital hepatic fibrosis

nodes.description: High blood pressure, affecting 70-80% of ADPKD patients, often preceding decline in renal function.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000822 | Hypertension | Frequent (79-30%)
Hypertension affects 70% to 80% of patients with ADPKD
Kidney manifestations include early-onset hypertension, kidney pain, and kidney insufficiency.

nodes.description: Cerebral berry aneurysms occur in 9-14% of ADPKD patients, with fivefold increased risk compared to general population.

evidence.snippet: HP:0004944 | Dilatation of the cerebral artery | Occasional (29-5%)
approximately 9% to 14% develop intracranial aneurysms, which have a rupture rate of 0.57 per 1000 patient-years
the prevalence of intracranial aneurysms is fivefold higher than in the general population and further increased in those with a positive family history of aneurysms or subarachnoid hemorrhage

nodes.description: Cardiac valvular abnormality reported in ADPKD patients as an extrarenal manifestation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001634 | Mitral valve prolapse | Occasional (29-5%)
dilatation of the aortic root and dissection of the thoracic aorta; mitral valve prolapse; and abdominal wall hernias

nodes.description: Multiple fluid-filled cysts in both kidneys, progressively enlarging over time.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000107 | Renal cyst | Very frequent (99-80%)
Autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease (ADPKD) is characterized by progressive development of kidney cysts
the vasopressin V2-receptor antagonist tolvaptan slowed the growth in total kidney volume

nodes.description: Kidney stones occur in 20-28% of ADPKD patients, including uric acid and calcium oxalate stones.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000791 | Uric acid nephrolithiasis | Occasional (29-5%)
HP:0008672 | Calcium oxalate nephrolithiasis | Occasional (29-5%)
Treatment of nephrolithiasis is standard.

nodes.description: Reduced amniotic fluid from impaired fetal renal function in severe ARPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001562 | Oligohydramnios | Frequent (79-30%)
Potter-sequence, oligohydramnios, pulmonary hypoplasia, and massively enlarged echogenic kidneys

nodes.description: Cysts in the pancreas occur as an extrarenal manifestation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001737 | Pancreatic cysts | Occasional (29-5%)
cysts in the pancreas, seminal vesicles, and arachnoid membrane

nodes.description: Bilateral cystic dilatation and ectasia of renal collecting tubules, a defining renal feature of ARPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000113 | Polycystic kidney dysplasia | Very frequent (99-80%)
characterized by cystic dilatation and ectasia of renal collecting tubules

nodes.description: Elevated portal venous pressure secondary to congenital hepatic fibrosis in ARPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001409 | Portal hypertension | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Underdeveloped lungs due to oligohydramnios from severe fetal kidney disease.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002089 | Pulmonary hypoplasia | Frequent (79-30%)
extreme bilateral enlargement of cystic kidneys in utero associated with hepatic ductal plate abnormalities and pulmonary hypoplasia

nodes.description: Kidney infections that may be complicated by cyst infections in ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012330 | Pyelonephritis | Occasional (29-5%)
Comorbidities include hypertension, flank pain, and bacterial infections.

nodes.description: Urinary tract and cyst infections are a significant complication, sometimes difficult to treat.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000010 | Recurrent urinary tract infections | Occasional (29-5%)
characterized by progressive outgrowths of fluid-filled cysts from the renal epithelium, which can manifest with hematuria, urinary tract infections, hypertension, and abdominal or flank pain
Treatment of cyst infections is difficult, with a high failure rate.

nodes.description: Seminal vesicle cysts and reduced sperm motility reported in male ADPKD patients.

evidence.snippet: HP:0012207 | Reduced sperm motility | Occasional (29-5%)
cysts in the pancreas, seminal vesicles, and arachnoid membrane

readouts.interpretation: Height-adjusted total kidney volume is an imaging readout of cumulative cyst burden and progressive kidney enlargement; larger and faster-growing TKV predicts steeper eGFR decline and earlier end-stage renal disease, supporting TKV as a prognostic enrichment biomarker for ADPKD trial patient selection, not a validated treatment-efficacy surrogate.

nodes.description: Elevated vasopressin signaling via V2 receptors increases cAMP in tubular epithelial cells, driving chloride and fluid secretion into cysts and promoting cyst wall growth. Blocking V2 signaling slows total kidney volume expansion.

evidence.reference_title: Tolvaptan in Later-Stage Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease.
evidence.snippet: the vasopressin V2-receptor antagonist tolvaptan slowed the growth in total kidney volume
